tasks.title: Domain plain-struct models (Coordinate, RouteEndpoint, obstacle enums, ObstacleReport, RoutePolyline, RouteCandidate, RouteSnapshot/Source, ObstacleRelationship, ObstacleMatch, ConfidenceLevel, FrictionCategory, ScoredRoute, RouteRecommendation, ReportDraft, Favorite, RecentDestination)
acceptance_criteria: All types match interfaces-json signatures exactly, Sendable/Codable/Equatable as specified
Zero imports beyond Foundation and CoreLocation-for-math
Compiles standalone with no SwiftData or MapKit dependency

tasks.title: Scoring engine: ScoringConstants, RouteScorer, ObstacleMatcher
acceptance_criteria: outerBufferMeters=20, innerBufferMeters=10 as fixed constants
recencyMultiplier bands: <7d=1.0, 8-30d=0.6, 31-90d=0.3, >90d=0.1, daysOld is Int
RouteScorer.recommend applies shorter-route tie-break on equal friction category
ObstacleMatcher bounding-box pre-filters before per-segment projection math

tasks.title: Domain boundary-value unit tests
acceptance_criteria: Asserts 19m counts / 21m does not
Asserts 9m reads on-route / 11m reads nearby
Asserts day 6 vs 8, day 30 vs 31, day 90 vs 91 land in different decay bands
Asserts shorter route wins on equal friction category tie
This suite is a required merge gate for any change under Domain/Scoring/

tasks.title: Persistence force-quit and CloudKit-schema-safety spikes
acceptance_criteria: Two independent ModelContainer instances against the same store URL surface a committed write across instances
A throwaway ModelConfiguration with cloudKitDatabase: .automatic does not throw against the planned schema shape
Findings documented before T-005 begins; entitlement stays off in shipped code

tasks.title: SwiftData records, ModelContainerFactory, domain<->record mapping
acceptance_criteria: Every attribute has a default, relationships optional, no unique constraints, explicit stable UUID fields
ModelContainerFactory produces containers usable by the two-instance survival pattern proven in T-004

tasks.title: ObstacleStore repository + DemoDataSeeder
acceptance_criteria: installSeedDataIfNeeded checks ScoringConstants.currentSeedDataVersion against a persisted flag, no duplicate rows on repeat install
resetSeedData backs the Settings demo-data-reset control
supersede and supersedeNearby implemented per ObstacleStore protocol
Seed corridor uses invented street names inside a real named city

tasks.title: FavoritesStore, RecentsStore, CachedRouteStore repositories
acceptance_criteria: FavoritesStore.count() usable before add() so a 3rd-favorite check never half-saves
RecentsStore caps at 5, label+coordinate only, clearRecents implemented
CachedRouteStore honors ~1 day staleness threshold and implements deleteExpiredRoutes(olderThan:)

tasks.title: Store-level supersession and force-quit persistence integration tests
acceptance_criteria: Save an obstacle, submit a new report inside the inner 10m buffer, assert fetchActiveReports excludes the old row while fetchHistory returns both
Force-quit survival verified via two independent ModelContainer instances against the same store URL for all four stores
This suite is a required merge gate for any change under Persistence/

tasks.title: LocationService, RouteSearchService (RouteFetching/PlaceSearching), ReachabilityMonitor
acceptance_criteria: LocationService exposes authorizationState as observed state, not polled; When-In-Use only
RouteSearchService wraps MKDirections/MKLocalSearch and throws only RouteRequestError, never raw MKError
ReachabilityMonitor is NWPathMonitor-backed and used only as a coarse hint, never sole error-cause truth

tasks.title: PurchaseService + StoreKit local configuration
acceptance_criteria: Non-consumable product id com.clearpathaccess.pro.lifetime
hasProUnlock reflects on-device VerificationResult only, no receipt-validation backend
purchasePro and restorePurchases both testable against the local .storekit config

tasks.title: ReportSubmission decision logic + tests
acceptance_criteria: Chooses supersede(existingID:) when targetObstacleID is set, else supersedeNearby using innerBufferMeters (10m, not 20m), else saveReport
Test proves a two-doors-down unrelated obstacle is not superseded by a new report

tasks.title: App shell, composition root, RootView with fake dependencies
acceptance_criteria: Exactly one AppDependencies bundle built once and injected via environment
Per-tab NavigationStacks for Plan and Saved, independent path types
App builds and runs end-to-end against fakes with no real service implementation yet

tasks.title: Design system tokens and shared component inventory (frozen milestone-1 deliverable so the core flow is never blocked on a later-starting lane)
acceptance_criteria: Colors/spacing/radius/type match locked tokens exactly, no color reused for a second meaning
Every custom control has explicit accessibilityLabel/accessibilityValue, none left to default inference
44x44pt minimum tap target on every interactive component
Owned by primary_ui; all other lanes consume it read-only, new-component requests go through primary_ui as a handoff, same discipline as AppDependencies

tasks.title: RouteExplanation types, RouteExplanationBuilder, first-run flag utility
acceptance_criteria: RouteExplanationBuilder.build is a pure static function producing the what/why/confidence triad plus the honest single-route wording branch
hasCompletedFirstRouteSearch is UserDefaults-backed and never derived from zero-recents/zero-favorites state
Pulled to the front of this lane's queue since Route Result (milestone 3) depends on it, not deferred to this lane's milestone-5 work

tasks.title: Onboarding + Plan tab
acceptance_criteria: Onboarding is 3 cards max ending in a location-permission step with an equal-weight manual-entry path
Plan has no map surface, ever
Fresh-install demo card appears only when hasCompletedFirstRouteSearch is false, gated by the flag from T-014, never by recents/favorites emptiness

tasks.title: Destination/origin search screen
acceptance_criteria: Manual pin-drop entry is first-class, not a fallback
Empty/ambiguous search results have distinct, honest copy

tasks.title: Route Result screen: two-stage loading, map/list, RouteResultViewModel and nav types
acceptance_criteria: Single scrolling container, no fixed header, survives largest Dynamic Type with no truncation
checkingObstacles phase holds a visible 400-600ms minimum even when local scoring is instant
Single-route results hide switcher chips and use honest only-route-found wording
Zero-matched-obstacle routes say no reported obstacles, never safe or accessible

tasks.title: Route Result loading-floor timing test and scenario state tests
acceptance_criteria: Mechanical test asserts checkingObstacles duration meets the 400-600ms floor even with instant local scoring
Distinct honestly-worded tests exist for permission-denied, offline, geocoding failure, no-route-found, no-nearby-obstacles, low-confidence, single-route

tasks.title: Report Obstacle sheet + Obstacle Detail sheet + local rescore wiring
acceptance_criteria: Report completes in under 30 seconds
Save calls ReportSubmission.submit then routeResultViewModel.rescore() only, never a second directions call
looks clear now option appears only when reporting against an existing flagged item

tasks.title: Manual VoiceOver pass on Route Result and Report sheet
acceptance_criteria: VoiceOver reaches route summary, every flagged item, and the report action with zero dead ends
Reading order matches recommendation -> confidence -> why -> selector -> toggle -> content

tasks.title: Saved tab: favorites list, lazy staggered refresh capped at 2 concurrent (owned by primary_ui — FavoriteRowStatus and SavedRoute were locked as primary_ui types in the tech-spec interfaces-json)
acceptance_criteria: Rows refetch lazily and staggered, never all at once on tab appear
Persistent 2-of-2-free indicator shown at cap
Reopened favorite uses today's suggested route framing, live re-fetch of geometry only

tasks.title: Saved refresh concurrency-safety test
acceptance_criteria: Test proves no race or double-fire when two favorite rows trigger a refresh close together, not just visual staggering

tasks.title: Paywall sheet + StoreKit purchase/restore UI + restore-gates-paywall test
acceptance_criteria: 3rd favorite save attempt triggers paywall over the actual route just tried, not a silent failure
Sells only unlimited favorites plus share export, never implies routing/scoring/reporting are premium
Test asserts restoring purchases after reinstall suppresses the paywall on the very next favorite-save attempt, distinct from restore-works-in-isolation

tasks.title: Share snapshot builder + share preview + ShareLink export
acceptance_criteria: ShareSnapshot is self-contained, no account or network required to open
Share preview shows exactly what ShareLink will export before it's invoked

tasks.title: Settings/info screen
acceptance_criteria: Full disclaimer text, sample-data explanation, restore purchases, note-privacy guidance, demo-data reset, and clear-recents controls all present and functional

tasks.title: Integration pass: wire real services into AppDependencies for release configuration
acceptance_criteria: Real LocationService, RouteSearchService, ObstacleRepository, FavoritesRepository, RecentsRepository, CachedRouteRepository, PurchaseService, ReachabilityMonitor all wired in place of fakes
Fakes remain available for previews/tests only, gated out of the release build
This file plus target build-settings/scheme changes are the only edits requiring integrator sign-off; routine new-file target-membership additions by any lane are not a shared-edit-zone violation

tasks.title: Accessibility CI gate wiring
acceptance_criteria: performAccessibilityAudit() runs as a mechanical CI gate covering contrast, hit-target size, missing labels across all screens
Runs alongside, not instead of, the manual VoiceOver pass from T-020

tasks.title: Full regression script and final release checklist
acceptance_criteria: Script covers fresh install, demo corridor, manual origin, zero-obstacle route, low-confidence route, report submission, favorite save, paywall intercept, cached-route offline review, and seed reset
Every step passes on a real device or simulator run before the app is called done
